provenance: REAL humpback (Megaptera novaeangliae) DTAG deployment 'mn09_203a' ('lavaliers_Calf'), baked from the operator's external H5 /Users/gilraitses/whale-behavior-analysis/Visualization_Poster_Appendix/data/dive_analysis.h5 by dev/bake_humpback_contrast.py using prebake.py primitives. Role: CONTRAST baseline only. This is NOT an orca driver; the orca twin is never driven by humpback motion and humpback kinematics are never presented as orca.

datum.name: NAVD88-0m
datum.note: depth_m is positive-down metres. The web applies Y = -depth_m * worldUnitsPerMeter at the live fit value; vertical placement is NOT baked in.

channels.name: t_s
channels.desc: Sample time on the common time base, t = i / sample_rate_hz.

channels.name: body_yaw_rad
channels.frame: true heading; NED 0=North, increasing clockwise toward East
channels.sign: Stored as declination-corrected TRUE heading in radians. Web maps to a rotation about scene +Y so the rostrum (+X) points along the heading (SKELETON.md section 5: +Y up, +X forward, +Z port).

channels.name: body_pitch_rad
channels.sign: positive = nose-up (tagtools a2pr convention, SKELETON.md section 3/5)

channels.name: body_roll_rad
channels.sign: positive = dorsal surface banks toward +Z (port/left bank); full +/-pi range

channels.name: depth_m
channels.sign: Web places the root at Y = -depth_m * worldUnitsPerMeter using the LIVE fit value (DEFAULT_WORLD_UNITS_PER_METER or the per-fit value the scene attaches), via setDepthPose. Not scaled here.

channels.name: fluke_phase_rad
channels.frame: dorso-ventral, pitch-axis (Z) on the caudal chain
channels.sign: Instantaneous phase of the band-passed dorso-ventral (heave, Az) oscillation. Continuous so the beat stays smooth between samples.
channels.source: Hilbert phase of band-passed animal-frame Az (static/gravity removed)

channels.name: fluke_amplitude
channels.frame: scales the per-joint amplitude profile (0 = no beat, 1 = full nominal stroke)
channels.sign: Envelope (DBA) of the band-passed Az oscillation, normalized to 0..1.
channels.source: Hilbert envelope of band-passed animal-frame Az

notes: role=contrast: baseline for the orca-vs-humpback table; NOT the orca driver.
Orientation is the H5's already-derived pitch/roll/head product (radians).
Fluke beat extracted from the whale-frame dorso-ventral Aw.3 (Az) channel with a humpback stroke band [0.12, 0.6] Hz (slower than the orca 0.3-1.0 Hz band).
Validates prebake.py's bin/JSON format and Az-band fluke method on real per-sample animaltags data.

fluke_beat.method: Az-band (Aw.3) dominant frequency + tag stroke-peak cross-check
fluke_beat.az_band_spectral_caveat: Whole-record FFT is biased low by intermittent stroking/glides; use the stroke-peak rate as the headline fluke-beat frequency.
